chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Central line required. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose 450mg/m² (400mg/m² if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Vesicant.

Do not start until urinary pH above 7. Requires folinic acid rescue 30mg every 3 hours IV starting 24 hours after start of infusion, continued until methotrexate level below 0.1 micromol/L. Creatinine clearance must be ≥50ml/min. Presence of third fluid compartment (ascites/pleural effusion) may delay clearance and increase toxicity.

pre_medications.frequency: three times daily for 3 days then when required
every 3 hours starting 24 hours after start of methotrexate infusion, until methotrexate level below 0.1 micromol/L
once only when required for fluid overload or to maintain urine output

dose_modifications.condition: < 0.75 x10⁹/L on day 1 of doxorubicin cycle
dose_modifications.notes: Delay 3-4 days until neutrophils ≥0.75x10⁹/L. Continue at full dose unless previous dose reduction. For repeated delay >7 days use growth factors rather than dose reduce.

dose_modifications.condition: < 75 x10⁹/L on day 1 of doxorubicin cycle

dose_modifications.notes: Methotrexate should only be delayed (no dose reductions apply) until recovery.
Methotrexate should only be delayed (no dose reductions apply) until recovery.
Reduce doxorubicin to 75% of original dose.
Reduce doxorubicin to 50% of original dose.
Reduce doxorubicin to 25% of original dose.
Consider dose reduction in severe renal failure.
Creatinine clearance of ≥50ml/min required to proceed with methotrexate.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: For doxorubicin. Methotrexate: delay if neutrophils <0.25x10⁹/L or platelets <50x10⁹/L. Creatinine clearance ≥50ml/min required for methotrexate.

LVEF baseline in patients with cardiac history/risk factors or elderly
GFR by EDTA or 24-hour urine collection prior to methotrexate infusion
Methotrexate levels every 24 hours starting 24 hours after end of infusion until <0.1 micromol/L
Urinary pH every 2 hours minimum until methotrexate level <0.1 micromol/L
Strict fluid balance chart throughout methotrexate administration

Significant drug interactions with IV methotrexate – always check before prescribing additional medication

A central line must be in place to administer doxorubicin
Methotrexate infusion must not start until urinary pH >7

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 1-12: days 1 and 4. Cycles 13-36: day 1 only. Total 48 infusions over 36 weeks. Dose rounded to nearest 0.08mg (up if halfway). Neutral for extravasation.

dose_modifications.notes: Please discuss with consultant. Dose modifications dependent on clinical circumstances and treatment intent.

Increased risk of ototoxicity if combined with cisplatin

Hypersensitivity: interrupt for minor symptoms; immediately discontinue for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).

Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with first or second infusion

indication: Histologically confirmed metastatic or unresectable gastrointestinal stromal tumour (GIST) following disease progression on or intolerance to imatinib and sunitinib

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Available as 40mg tablets. Take at same time each day, swallowed whole with water after a light meal containing <30% fat. Avoid grapefruit juice. Lowest recommended daily dose 80mg. Dose modifications in 40mg steps. NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed.

dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt; monitor weekly; restart with 40mg dose reduction if benefit outweighs hepatotoxicity risk; monitor LFTs weekly for ≥4 weeks. Re-occurrence: permanently discontinue.
Delay until NCI-CTC grade ≤1; reduce dose by one level (40mg). Dose reduction levels: level 1 = 120mg/day, level 2 = 80mg/day, level 3 = 40mg/day.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to cycle 1. No dose adjustment required for renal impairment. Not recommended in severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C). No treatment breaks >6 weeks beyond expected cycle length.

monitoring_requirements: ALT, AST and bilirubin at baseline then at least every 2 weeks during first 2 months then every 4 weeks or as clinically indicated
U&Es including serum phosphate, calcium, sodium and potassium
Blood pressure at baseline and then every cycle
Thyroid function at baseline then every 12 weeks or as clinically indicated
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed
Must only be prescribed under supervision of consultant oncologist
Regorafenib interacts with many other agents – always check for drug interactions
Skin reactions (palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia): detailed dose modification schedule based on NCI-CTC grade and occurrence number
Avoid grapefruit juice

drugs.special_instructions: Available as 40mg tablets. Take at same time each day, swallowed whole with water after a light meal containing <30% fat. Avoid grapefruit juice. Lowest recommended daily dose 80mg. Dose modifications in 40mg steps. NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed.
drugs.notes: once daily days 1-21 then 7-day break | Available as 40mg tablets. Take at same time each day, swallowed whole with water after a light meal containing <30% fat. Avoid grapefruit juice. Lowest recommended daily dose 80mg. Dose modifications in 40mg steps. NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed.

indication: Unresectable or metastatic malignant gastrointestinal stromal tumours (GIST) after imatinib failure or intolerance; WHO Performance status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps. Minimum dose 25mg/day. NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed. Must be prescribed under oncologist/haematologist supervision. Check drug interactions.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery to ≥1x10⁹/L. If recovery within 7 days continue at last dose. If recovery >7 days reduce by 12.5mg.
Delay until recovery to ≥75x10⁹/L. If recovery within 7 days continue at full dose. If recovery >7 days reduce by 12.5mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Child-Pugh A or B hepatic impairment
dose_modifications.notes: 50mg daily – no dose reduction required for Child-Pugh A or B. No information for Child-Pugh C.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps. Minimum dose 25mg/day. No dose adjustment required for renal impairment.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle for first 3 cycles (may reduce to every other cycle if stable)
Blood pressure weekly for first 4 weeks then every 6-12 weeks
Thyroid function tests at baseline then every 3 months
Baseline LVEF in patients with cardiac history or elderly; repeat every 3-6 months as clinically indicated
special_warnings: NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert must be followed
Must be prescribed under supervision of oncologist/haematologist
Sunitinib interacts with many other agents – always check drug interactions
Hypothyroidism: manage per standard practice, no need to dose reduce sunitinib
Palmar-plantar erythrodysaesthesia: grade 1 – treat symptomatically; grade 2 – delay and reduce 12.5mg; grade 3 – delay until grade 1 then reduce 12.5-25mg
Hypertension >140/90mmHg despite treatment: reduce dose in 12.5mg steps

drugs.special_instructions: Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps. Minimum dose 25mg/day. NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed. Must be prescribed under oncologist/haematologist supervision. Check drug interactions.
drugs.notes: once daily days 1-28 then 14-day break | Available as 12.5mg, 25mg, 37.5mg and 50mg capsules. Dose modifications in 12.5mg steps. Minimum dose 25mg/day. NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed. Must be prescribed under oncologist/haematologist supervision. Check drug interactions.

indication: Advanced soft tissue sarcoma where treatment with anthracyclines and ifosfamide has failed, or they cannot be tolerated or are unsuitable

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Vesicant. Central venous access strongly recommended – peripheral administration carries risk of severe injection site reaction. Continuous infusion at 10ml/hour. First dose reduction 1.2mg/m², second dose reduction 1.0mg/m².

pre_medications.frequency: once, 30 minutes prior to trabectedin infusion

take_home_medicines.frequency: twice daily for 1 day on the day before trabectedin infusion (for next cycle)

dose_modifications.condition: < 0.5 x10⁹/L lasting >5 days or with fever/infection between cycles
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce by one dose level for subsequent cycles.
Reduce by one dose level for subsequent cycles.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin above ULN prior to treatment
dose_modifications.notes: Bilirubin must be within normal range before treatment. Delay up to 3 weeks.

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin rises above ULN between cycles

dose_modifications.notes: AST and ALT must be <2.5xULN before treatment. Also alkaline phosphatase ≤2.5xULN and albumin >25g/L and CPK <2.5xULN required.

dose_modifications.condition: AST or ALT >2.5xULN not recovered by day 21 between cycles
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce by one dose level for subsequent cycles.
Trabectedin monotherapy may only be used if creatinine clearance ≥30ml/min.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Also requires: bilirubin within normal range, ALP ≤2.5xULN, albumin >25g/L, ALT/AST <2.5xULN, CPK <2.5xULN. Delay up to 3 weeks; if criteria not met treatment should be discontinued.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of treatment
Haematological parameters, bilirubin, alkaline phosphatase, aminotransferases and CPK weekly during first 2 cycles, then at least once between treatments in subsequent cycles
special_warnings: Central venous access strongly recommended – peripheral administration risk of severe injection site reaction
Once dose reduced due to toxicity, dose escalation in subsequent cycles is not recommended
Delay treatment for up to 3 weeks if re-treatment criteria not met

drugs.special_instructions: Vesicant. Central venous access strongly recommended – peripheral administration carries risk of severe injection site reaction. Continuous infusion at 10ml/hour. First dose reduction 1.2mg/m², second dose reduction 1.0mg/m².
drugs.notes: Vesicant. Central venous access strongly recommended – peripheral administration carries risk of severe injection site reaction. Continuous infusion at 10ml/hour. First dose reduction 1.2mg/m², second dose reduction 1.0mg/m².
